titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/234

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=499 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-12 13:45:36.011+00 | translation moa=500 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-12 13:45:35.983215+00

p.cl:
Omnibus Christi fidelibus tam presentibus quam futuris ad quos presens Scriptum peruenerit Magister Willelmus de Grenelau filius Rolandi
filius Willelmi salutem in domino .

vniuersitati vestre notum facio me diuine pietatis intuitu et pro salute anime mee et pro salute
animarum omnium predecessorum et successorum meorum dedisse et concessisse et hac carta mea confirmasse deo et ecclesie beate Marie de
Melros et monachis ibidem deo et matri sue seruientibus et inperpetuum seruituris tres carucatas terre quas habui in territorio
ville de halsingtun cum omnibus pertinenciis suis illas scilicet quas domina Matildis mater Domini Roberti de Muscampo dedit
Willelmo filio Iohannis de honum cum Gilia filia dicte Matildis in liberum Maritagium Tenendas et habendas dictis Monachis
de Melros cum omnibus libertatibus aisiamentis et Iuribus ad eas pertinentibus inperpetuum in liberam puram et perpetuam elemosinam
nullum omnino seculare seruicium pro eis alicui faciendo preter oraciones et suffragia et alia beneficia deo placentia que
dicti monachi promiserunt in fide quam deo debent et ordini suo se facturos pro me inperpetuum

Et ego et heredes mei
manutenebimus predictis Monachis hanc elemosinam et warantizabimus contra omnes homines et adquietabimus eam de omni
seruicio inperpetuum
In cuius Rei testimonium sigillum meum presentibus est appensum

hiis testibus Radulpho de Bonekil
Iohanne de letham Iohanne pepedi Dauid de Buredon halano de harcar’ Iohanne filio Simonis Iohanne de lembeden’
Roberto filio suo . Rolando filio Iohannis . et multis aliis .
To all Christ's faithful as well present as future to whom the present writing shall come, Master William of Greenlaw son of Roland sends greeting in the Lord.

I make known to your whole community that moved by divine love and for the salvation of my soul and for the salvation of the souls of all my predecessors and successors I have given and granted and by this my charter made firm to God and to the church of Blessed Mary at Melrose and to the monks in that place serving God and their Mother and who in perpetuity will serve there three ploughgates of land that I had in the territory of the toun of Hassington with all its pertinents; that is, those that the lady Matilda, mother of the lord Robert de Muschamp, gave to William, son of John of Hownam, with Gilia, daughter of the said Matilda, in free marriage; for the said monks to hold and have with all liberties, easements, and rights belonging to it for ever, in free, pure, and everlasting alms; by performing no secular service whatever for them to anyone, except for prayers and sugffrages and other spiritual goods which the said monks promised in faith, as much as they owe to God and their own Order, that they would perform for me for ever.
And I and my heirs shall maintain to the said monks this alms, and we shall warrant it against all men, and we shall acquit it in respect of every service for ever.
In testimony of this thing my seal has been applied to these presents.
With these witnesses: Ralph of Bunkle; John of Latham; John of Papedy; David of Burradon; Alan of Harcarse; John son of Simon; John of Lambden; Robert his son; Roland son of John; and many others.
